title: Pan Seared American Tofu Skillet
description: Savory and shattering American dish featuring buttermilk and sashimi-cut tofu, perfect for a vegan-friendly brunch, this skillet is a delicious combination of flavors and textures. It is made with a variety of ingredients and is easy to prepare.
instructions: Cut the extra firm tofu into small cubes and place them in a shallow dish.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, all purpose flour, cornstarch, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the buttermilk mixture over the tofu and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat.
Remove the tofu from the marinade and add it to the skillet.
Cook the tofu for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until it is golden brown and crispy.
Remove the tofu from the skillet and set it aside on a plate.
Reduce the heat to medium and add any remaining marinade to the skillet.
Scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet and bring the marinade to a simmer.
Return the tofu to the skillet and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the tofu is fully coated.
